identity.name: AI Access — Source Code Paste to Public ChatGPT
identity.summary: A developer pastes proprietary source code into the public OpenAI ChatGPT API. The llm_provider_egress plugin POSTs a marked source snippet (CORTEXSIM-CANARY) to api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions; Cortex AI Access Security and the NGFW generative-AI App-ID flag the egress and the DLP source-code classifier matches the prompt body.
identity.description: Shadow-AI source-code disclosure is the most common GenAI data-loss pattern in the enterprise: an engineer pastes a confidential file into ChatGPT to refactor or debug it, exfiltrating intellectual property over an encrypted channel that bypasses traditional egress controls. This scenario emits authentic-shape HTTPS POSTs to api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions carrying a planted proprietary Python snippet tagged with the CORTEXSIM-CANARY marker. Detection is layered: (1) the NGFW classifies the destination as a generative-AI application via App-ID on the outbound TLS session; (2) Cortex AI Access Security DLP inspects the decrypted prompt body and matches source-code structure markers; (3) an analytics rule profiles repeated source-paste bursts from a single user. A correlation rule fuses the App-ID match, the DLP content hit and the burst pattern into a single Shadow-AI data-exfiltration incident. Every request carries an X-Simulation-Run-ID header so the SOC can filter the simulated traffic.

prerequisites.description: Cortex AI Access Security licensed and inline (NGFW App-ID + DLP decrypt on the egress path).
prerequisites.check_command: python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli describe llm_provider_egress | jq -e '.eal_targets | length > 0'

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-AIACC-001 requirements (egress allowed to api.openai.com).

payload.code:
# step-1: Pre-flight: confirm llm_provider_egress plugin is registered
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli describe llm_provider_egress \
  | jq -e '.eal_targets | length > 0'
# step-2: Single source-code POST to api.openai.com via EAL plugin
cat > /tmp/aiacc-001.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-AIACC-001
name: "AI Access — source paste to OpenAI"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - api.openai.com
dry_run: false
tags: [ai-access, openai, source-paste]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: llm_provider_egress
    params:
      provider: openai
      payload_type: source
      iterations: 1
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/aiacc-001.yml --live
# step-3: Repeated POSTs (x3) to confirm stable per-session detection
cat > /tmp/aiacc-001-burst.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-AIACC-001
name: "AI Access — source paste burst"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - api.openai.com
dry_run: false
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: llm_provider_egress
    params:
      provider: openai
      payload_type: source
      iterations: 3
      sleep_seconds: 5
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/aiacc-001-burst.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Outbound HTTPS POST to api.openai.com on the chat-completions path.
Prompt body carries the source-code canary marker.
Every simulated request carries an X-Simulation-Run-ID header for SOC filtering.

biocs.name: AIACC-001 Pre-flight LLM Provider Egress Plugin Registered
biocs.description: Pre-flight: plugin metadata visible to operator. Confirms the llm_provider_egress EAL plugin is registered before the scenario emits any provider traffic; matches the describe/registration audit event on the simulation worker.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("python", "python3")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "scripts.eal_simulator.cli"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "describe"
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains "llm_provider_egress"

xql_queries.name: AIACC-001 Source Code Shape POST to OpenAI With Canary Marker
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "api.openai.com" or tls_sni = "api.openai.com"
| filter http_method = "POST" and uri_path contains "/v1/chat/completions"
| filter request_body contains "CORTEXSIM-CANARY" or request_body contains "def settle_transactions"
| fields _time, source_user, src_ip, dst_host, uri_path, bytes_sent, app

xql_queries.name: AIACC-001 DLP Source Code Structure Regex Match in LLM Prompt
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host in ("api.openai.com", "api.anthropic.com", "generativelanguage.googleapis.com")
| filter request_body ~= "(?:^|\n)\s*(?:def |class |import |from\s+\w+\s+import|#\s*CORTEXSIM-CANARY)"
| fields _time, source_user, src_ip, dst_host, uri_path, bytes_sent

xql_queries.name: AIACC-001 NGFW Generative AI App-ID Outbound HTTPS to OpenAI
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "api.openai.com" or tls_sni = "api.openai.com"
| filter app in ("openai-api", "chatgpt", "generative-ai", "ai-ml-application")
| fields _time, source_user, src_ip, dst_host, app, app_category, bytes_sent, action

xql_queries.name: AIACC-001 Repeated Source Paste Burst to OpenAI Single User
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_host = "api.openai.com"
| filter http_method = "POST" and uri_path contains "/v1/chat/completions"
| comp count() as post_count, sum(bytes_sent) as total_bytes by source_user, src_ip
| filter post_count >= 3
| fields source_user, src_ip, post_count, total_bytes

correlation_rules.name: Shadow-AI Source Code Disclosure to OpenAI
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(AIACC-001 Pre-flight LLM Provider Egress Plugin Registered) OR XQL(AIACC-001 Source Code Shape POST to OpenAI With Canary Marker) OR XQL(AIACC-001 DLP Source Code Structure Regex Match in LLM Prompt) OR XQL(AIACC-001 NGFW Generative AI App-ID Outbound HTTPS to OpenAI) OR XQL(AIACC-001 Repeated Source Paste Burst to OpenAI Single User)

analytics_modules: Cortex AI Access Security — Generative-AI App-ID classification
Cortex AI Access Security — Source-code DLP content inspection

products.evidence_query: dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter dst_host = "api.openai.com" and request_body contains "CORTEXSIM-CANARY" | fields _time, source_user, dst_host, uri_path, bytes_sent
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter app in ("openai-api","chatgpt","generative-ai") | fields _time, source_user, dst_host, app, action
Correlation CR-AIACC-0001 fuses the App-ID match, source-code DLP hit and repeated-paste burst into one Shadow-AI data-exfiltration incident.

use_cases.name: Shadow-AI Source Code Disclosure
use_cases.description: Detect proprietary source code pasted into the public OpenAI ChatGPT API and stitch App-ID, DLP and burst signals into one incident.

test_cases.objective: A single source-code paste to api.openai.com fires the generative-AI App-ID and the source-code DLP classifier.
preconditions: Cortex AI Access Security inline with TLS decrypt on the egress path.
Source-code DLP profile and generative-AI App-ID enabled.
steps: Observe the App-ID and DLP alerts in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: XQL `AIACC-001 NGFW Generative AI App-ID Outbound HTTPS to OpenAI` returns >= 1 row.
XQL `AIACC-001 Source Code Shape POST to OpenAI With Canary Marker` returns >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Repeated source pastes from one user stitch into a single Shadow-AI incident.
success_criteria: XQL `AIACC-001 Repeated Source Paste Burst to OpenAI Single User` returns >= 1 row.
Correlation `CR-AIACC-0001` groups the signals into one incident.

preventive_controls: Enable Cortex AI Access Security source-code DLP in Block mode for unsanctioned generative-AI destinations.
Steer users to a sanctioned enterprise GenAI gateway and block direct egress to public provider APIs.
Apply generative-AI App-ID egress policy with TLS decrypt so prompt bodies are inspectable.
detection_engineering: Alert on POST bodies to LLM provider FQDNs that match source-code structure (def/class/import or canary markers).
Profile per-user request counts to each provider and alert on bursts above a rolling baseline.
Correlate App-ID classification with DLP content hits to confirm proprietary-code exfiltration intent.

references.title: Unit42 - Shadow AI in the Enterprise
MITRE ATT&CK T1567 — Exfiltration Over Web Service
